summary: An excerpt from Andrew Carnegie's Wikipedia page detailing his philosophical views on wealth and market concentration. It highlights his personal struggle against the idolatry of money and his economic belief that the concentration of capital is a necessary evolutionary step for societal development.

principles.statement: Choose a life that is elevating in character rather than solely focusing on making money.
principles.rationale: Being overwhelmed by business cares and the pursuit of wealth degrades a person and is a debasing form of idolatry.
principles.source_quote: Whatever I engage in I must push inordinately; therefore should I be careful to choose that life which will be the most elevating in its character.

principles.statement: The concentration of capital is a necessary and beneficial evolution for society.
principles.rationale: It meets the imperious demands of modern human society and represents an upward path of development from the heterogeneous to the homogeneous.
principles.source_quote: The concentration of capital is a necessity for meeting the demands of our day, and as such should not be looked at askance, but be encouraged.

mental_models.name: Economic Evolution
mental_models.description: Viewing economic trends, specifically the concentration of capital, as a natural evolutionary step upward from a disorganized (heterogeneous) state to an organized (homogeneous) state.
mental_models.example: Carnegie applied this to defend market concentration, arguing that the consolidation of wealth is not detrimental but a beneficial evolution of human society.

signature_quotes.text: Man must have no idol and the amassing of wealth is one of the worst species of idolatry ! No idol is more debasing than the worship of money!
signature_quotes.context: Written as a personal reflection on his career and the dangers of being consumed by business.

signature_quotes.text: To continue much longer overwhelmed by business cares and with most of my thoughts wholly upon the way to make more money in the shortest time, must degrade me beyond hope of permanent recovery.
signature_quotes.context: Reflecting on his plan to resign from business at thirty-five to pursue education and reading.

signature_quotes.text: It is an evolution from the heterogeneous to the homogeneous, and is clearly another step in the upward path of development.
signature_quotes.context: Defending the concentration of capital in 'The Gospel of Wealth'.

anti_patterns.description: Worshipping money and making the amassing of wealth one's primary focus in life.
anti_patterns.why_it_fails: It is a debasing form of idolatry that degrades a person's character beyond hope of permanent recovery.
